title: Server-side-only affiliate config and secrets handling
description: Define where affiliate partner IDs/tags/secrets live (config.yaml / environment) and enforce that they never reach the client, following the existing source_settings precedent for DB-stored per-source overrides while treating affiliate secrets as more sensitive than the current eBay credential handling.
acceptance_criteria: Affiliate config fields are documented in config.example.yaml with clear comments distinguishing them from source-definition fields
No affiliate config value is ever serialized into an HTML template, inline script, or JSON API response
No affiliate secret is logged in plaintext at any log level
config.yaml (gitignored) remains the source of truth for affiliate secrets; nothing affiliate-related is committed to the repo
A code review checklist item or test asserts /out/<listing_id> responses and page source contain no affiliate identifiers beyond the final destination URL itself
notes: See docs/adr/0002-affiliate-link-redirect-and-tracking.md, security assumption section. This is the feature most worth a second pair of eyes given it is the one place secrets and public-facing output meet in Phase 1. Shipped 2026-07-08: config.OutboundConfig.affiliate_params, documented in config.example.yaml. tests/test_web.py::test_affiliate_identifiers_never_appear_in_page_source asserts a configured campaign id string appears nowhere in dashboard/project/auctions/offers/api-status page source, only in the /out/<id> redirect's Location header. Also verified live via a real `python -m product_finder web` process + curl, not just the Flask test client.
